title: How snakebites push rural families deeper into poverty
content: The community-based retrospective study, conducted in Jagtial district of Telangana by researchers from the Hyderabad-based CSIR-Centre for Cellular and Molecular Biology (CCMB), highlights the deep and lasting impact of snakebites on agrarian households.

title: SIR explained: What Telangana voters need to know
content: As Telangana gears up for a door-to-door verification of voters, the Special Intensive Revision has emerged as one of the most closely watched electoral exercises in recent years. While election officials describe it as a routine effort to improve the accuracy of voter lists, political parties and electors have raised questions about implementation and the treatment of anomalies in voter data

title: Passenger’s leg gets stuck between metro train and platform; services disrupted briefly
content: The incident occurred between 10.20 a.m. and 10.30 a.m. at the Krantivira Sangolli Rayanna Railway Metro Station.

title: Despite grave concerns raised in the white paper, Kerala budget silent on bringing down committed expenditure
content: Committed expenditure is projected to exceed ₹1.22 lakh crore in 2026-27, with salaries, pensions and interest payments consuming nearly three-fourths of state revenues

title: Supreme Court declares right to walk on footpath a fundamental right, wants law
content: Apex court says walking powered the freedom struggle, politics and social reforms, but is yet to be recognised as a fundamental right; asks government to frame a law to protect pedestrians

title: World Refugee Day 2026: How refugees transformed Bengali food
content: From zero waste cooking and minimalist fish curries to Burmese noodle soups and Andaman settler kitchens, the flavours of displacement continue to shape what Bengal eats today.

title: Dongria Kondh of Niyamgiri: Where forests, food and faith shape daily life
content: An intimate glimpse into the lives of the Dongria Kondh, one of India’s Particularly Vulnerable Tribal Groups, in Odisha’s Niyamgiri hills

title: Renowned artist Bhaskara Rao Botcha to lead art workshop, public auction in Visakhapatnam
content: FICCI FLO Vijayawada Chapter will host contemporary artist Bhaskara Rao Botcha for an interactive art workshop and public art auction at Waltair Club, Visakhapatnam

title: Census 2027: The pressures of counting India
content: Enumerators working on the Census are facing several challenges in the field, including heat, connectivity issues, and safety concerns. Though the exercise is being conducted through a digital system designed for real-time monitoring, field workers say conditions on the ground and supervisory instructions are affecting data collection. Vijaita Singh reports on the challenges

title: EU passes law allowing offshore deportation centres
content: The Return Regulation approves the deportation of irregular immigrants to third-party countries; states can now form agreements with non-EU countries and set up deportation centres there; even as European Commission head Ursula von der Leyen hailed the law as ‘fair and firm’, critics say it has ‘little regard for people’s safety, dignity and rights, and fails to uphold the EU’s fundamental values’

title: Vivek Aggarwal appointed Financial Action Task Force vice-president
content: Currently serving as Secretary in Culture Ministry, Aggarwal’s selection is a significant development for India’s role in global financial governance and efforts to strengthen international cooperation on anti-money laundering, counter-terrorist financing and illicit financial flows

title: Emotional Uddhav offers to step down as Shiv Sena (UBT) president, Eknath Shinde hints at more defections
content: Mr. Thackeray calls for starting ‘Operation Lotus’ in response to ‘Operation Tiger’ allegedly carried out by the Shiv Sena to lure six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs into its fold and refuted allegations his party was likely to merge with the Congress

title: Supreme Court flags ‘brain drain’ in legal profession, directs creation of fund for young lawyers
content: The top court observed that the lack of a steady client base and limited remuneration in the early years of practice often result in acute financial hardship

title: BJP worker killed in Howrah; W.B. CM promises law to seize properties of accused
content: West Bengal Chief Minister Suvendu Adhikari asked people not to go to the houses of people accused of corruption and electoral violence and urged them to keep faith in the police and the administration to take stringent action in accordance with the law

title: Supreme Court refuses urgent hearing on plea to defer June 21 NEET-UG retest
content: The plea comes from 11 NEET aspirants who are not against a retest, but seek more time to prepare, given the “severe stress and anxiety” caused by the cancellation of the original exam and rumours of another paper leak

title: 3-language policy not viable, say CBSE schools in Nagaland
content: Principals from 19 schools urge Education Minister to grant the State a flexible framework as it is home to over 17 major recognised tribes each with their own distinct language and oral tradition

title: Police, probe agencies get app to scan fingerprints on the go
content: Abhigyan app will let police check thumb impressions collected on portable scanners against NAFIS database within seconds; this will strengthen ground-level policing, says Home Minister Amit Shah

title: Uttar Pradesh pushes comprehensive policy in fisheries sector eyeing rural prosperity
content: “Large water bodies (>2 hectares) are leased to cooperative societies to enable scalable operations, risk-sharing, and better governance”

title: M.P. to form dedicated task force to counter organised forest crime: CM Yadav
content: The Chief Minister also approved formation of a command and control centre for the protection of State’s forests and wildlife

title: 20 rebel Trinamool Lok Sabha MPs seek to merge with Nationalist Citizen Party of India
content: Meet Lok Sabha Speaker Om Birla and announce that they are merging with the registered regional party for time being.

title: President, PM to walk side by side in Odisha’s tribal hinterland
content: With India’s two tallest leaders set to share the stage, Odisha is in line for a host of development projects

title: Kerala Budget: New Budget has a long wish list, but not enough money allocated to make it a reality, says Thomas Isaac
content: The CPI(M) leader says Chief Minister V.D. Satheesan who had talked at length about resource mobilisation in his podcasts do not have any such measures spelled out in the Budget

title: Zero-fee admission for 1.85 lakh undergraduate students in Assam: CM
content: Himanta Biswa Sarma says the government’s fee-waiver scheme is ensuring seamless continuity in education

title: Former Odisha CM lashes out at current BJP govt for disruptions in disbursement of pensions
content: “It is important to recall that for the past 24 years, my government ensured that pensions were distributed regularly on the 15th of every month, observed as ‘Jan Seva Diwas,” says Naveen Patnaik

title: Kerala Budget: Budget announces industry benefits to tourism sector
content: According to the Budget, necessary legal amendments will be made to make more land available for tourism in the plantation sector

title: Senior Congress leader from Bihar writes to party top leardship ‘to check regional’ parties
content: “As a national party, Congress must be bold not only in confronting BJP but also in dealing with the opportunistic allies”

title: Kerala Budget: Planning to be abandoned, says Binoy Viswam
content: Revised Budget backtracks from welfare policies followed by the previous Left Democratic Front government, he says

title: No provision for Kerala to withdraw from PM SHRI deal: Chief Minister V. D. Satheesan
content: Chief Minister says only Union government had the right to withdraw from the PM SHRI agreement after giving a notice of one month. There was no provision in the agreement to keep the scheme implementation in abeyance either

title: SC says plea seeking CBI probe into May 13 Tamil Nadu trust vote based on ‘vague’ allegations
content: The top court said the plea was based on “vague, wild and casual allegations” and therefore did not merit interference

title: Uproar in Kaushambi over vandalism of Ambedkar’s statue
content: The opposition said the incidents were part of a nefarious design by feudal forces, supported by the BJP, to suppress and humiliate the Dalit community

title: CPI(M) claims poverty-stricken people are leaving Tripura to earn a living
content: He claimed nearly two lakh people have left the State under compulsion as there is no way to earn in the State

title: TVK, AIADMK, DMK and PMK claim credit for empowering SCs
content: Minister for Public Works Aadhav Arjuna appealed to parties to drop the term Thazhthapatta makkal (downtrodden people) and consider using a more neutral term — Scheduled Castes

title: Malabar River Festival: Kodenchery to host ‘Off-Road Fun Drive’
content: The event is being organised as part of the build-up to the 12th International White Water Kayaking Championship, a curtain-raiser to the Malabar River Festival

title: From Trump whisperer to Trump basher: Italy PM Meloni takes on US President
content: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni has boldly rejected Donald Trump's assertion that she pleaded for a selfie during a G7 summit, deeming his claims as completely 'fabricated.' She emphasized that neither herself nor Italy resorts to begging. This moment underlines Trump's unique diplomatic style, where he often embellishes relationships with global leaders like Emmanuel Macron and Mohammed bin Salman, spinning tales of admiration and desperation from foreign officials.

title: Watch: Qatar's $400 mn ‘world’s most luxurious plane’ joins Trump's Air Force One fleet
content: President Trump unveiled a $400 million Boeing 747 gifted by Qatar as a temporary Air Force One, sparking criticism over its value and potential impact on defense projects.  The luxurious jet, featuring a striking new livery, will serve until new presidential aircraft arrive in 2027-2028.  Trump defended the gift, citing the need to match foreign leaders' modern fleets and the retirement of an aging veteran aircraft.

title: 'We don't get to choose..': What Google CEO Sundar Pichai advices to students
content: Google CEO Sundar Pichai, addressing Stanford's Class of 2026, urged graduates to embrace optimism and tackle challenging endeavors.  He shared personal anecdotes, emphasizing that meaningful work stems from genuine passion rather than external expectations. Pichai's core message highlighted the importance of perseverance and continuous forward movement, regardless of life's inevitable uncertainties.

title: India's 2027 World Cup plans may hinge on Kuldeep rediscovering his rhythm
content: Kuldeep Yadav's place may not be under immediate threat, but his form has become a talking point. As India begin building towards the 2027 ODI World Cup, the team management is backing the left-arm wrist spinner to rediscover his wicket-taking touch and lead the middle overs once again.

title: A tale of two cross-votings: How power politics played out in Karnataka, Jharkhand
content: Cross-voting significantly impacted Rajya Sabha elections in Jharkhandand Legislative Council elections in Karnataka, with both the INDIA bloc and NDA facing losses. While parties lauded rival cross-voters as exercising conscience, they condemned their own MLAs for betraying the party. This highlights a persistent double standard, where power dynamics, not ideology, drive these electoral outcomes despite past reforms aimed at curbing corruption.

title: One of world’s richest persons & Louis Vuitton CEO on Elon Musk becoming trillionaire
content: Elon Musk has become the world's first trillionaire following SpaceX's historic public listing, with the company's valuation exceeding $2 trillion. Bernard Arnault, CEO of LVMH, acknowledged Musk's dominance in the rocket industry but clarified that the wealth represents company value, not liquid assets. Musk emphasized SpaceX's ambition to make humanity multi-planetary and space travel accessible to all.

title: Jeff Bezos’ ex-wife donates most of wealth to HBCUs after selling Amazon stake
content: MacKenzie Scott, a prominent philanthropist, has donated over $26 billion to 2,700 organizations, including significant contributions to Historically Black Colleges and Universities. Her trust-based approach involves unrestricted donations, empowering recipients to use funds freely. Despite her immense generosity, Scott's net worth remains substantial, and she remains committed to giving away her fortune.

title: Trump-Meloni selfie row: Italy foreign minister cancels US visit
content: Italian Foreign Minister Antonio Tajani has cancelled a US visit following controversial remarks by Donald Trump about Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. Trump claimed Meloni "begged" for a photo, a statement Meloni vehemently denied as "fabricated." The dispute has strained ties between the former allies, with Italian officials condemning Trump's "rants." The cancellation also postponed a key Italy-US business forum.

title: TMC, BJP clash at Kolkata airport as Abhishek Banerjee returns from Delhi
content: Chaos erupted outside Kolkata airport Friday night as Trinamool and BJP supporters clashed violently. BJP supporters allegedly planned to target Abhishek Banerjee with rotten eggs, prompting a swift counter-response from TMC cadres. The altercation, involving blows and hurled objects, caused panic among passengers and massive traffic jams. Banerjee left the airport under heavy police escort, avoiding interaction.

title: Ronaldo fans face backlash for targeting João Neves' girlfriend online
content: Portugal midfielder Joao Neves has found himself at the center of a major social media controversy following Portugal’s 1-1 draw against DR Congo in their opening FIFA World Cup match. The young midfielder scored Portugal’s only goal, but much of the post-match discussion shifted toward Cristiano Ronaldo’s performance. The veteran forward struggled to make an impact during the game, leading to criticism from football fans.

title: Toronto Strengthens Defense with Blockbuster $68M Lightning Acquisition
content: John Chayka did not ease into the Toronto Maple Leafs job. His first major swing brought Darren Raddysh from Tampa Bay in a reported $68 million sign-and-trade, giving Toronto a new power-play weapon and a clear defensive reset after a rough season with pressure attached to the price and term. Chayka now owns the bet. So does Toronto’s blue line.

title: Taylor Swift returns to studio ahead of Travis Kelce wedding surprise
content: Taylor Swift’s latest Electric Lady Studios visit has fueled fresh speculation about a private wedding surprise for Travis Kelce. Reports from Daily Mail, TMZ, Page Six, and HT point to a guarded celebration, rumored venue shifts, NDA talk, and one alleged gift that could turn their wedding into a pop-culture event. Nothing is confirmed yet by the couple.

title: The World Cup question Cristiano Ronaldo can no longer escape
content: Cristiano Ronaldo's effectiveness for Portugal is under intense scrutiny following a goalless draw against DR Congo. Despite his legendary status, the 41-year-old's limited touches and lack of decisive impact raise questions about his role in the 2026 World Cup. The team's attacking rhythm and statistics suggest a need for tactical evolution.

title: Pilots' organistaion casts doubt on interim Air India crash report, says AAIB must run simulator tests
content: Pilots' union FIP alleges the Aircraft Accident Investigation Bureau's report on Air India flight 171 omits crucial cockpit warnings of an electrical failure preceding engine loss. Independent simulator tests commissioned by FIP reportedly contradict the Bureau's timeline for emergency power deployment, raising serious questions about the investigation's findings and the fate of the deceased pilots.

title: At $4 billion, Jio IPO set to be India’s biggest ever
content: Jio Platforms, Reliance Industries' digital arm, is set to launch India's largest-ever IPO, aiming to raise up to $4 billion. The offering will be a primary share sale, with proceeds earmarked for debt reduction and corporate needs, marking a significant value-creation milestone for the company. This move signals a major event for the Indian stock market, with Jio expected to command a valuation exceeding $100 billion.

title: Explained: Why Harbhajan and Sreesanth are feuding again - Full timeline
content: Tensions between Harbhajan Singh and S Sreesanth have reignited, nearly 18 years after the infamous IPL 2008 slapgate. Sreesanth challenged Harbhajan to a fight, citing a recent advertisement he believes exploits the incident. Despite past reconciliations and apologies, Sreesanth feels the controversy, which deeply affected his family, is being revisited for financial gain, leading him to block the spinner.

title: Allu Arjun fulfils young leukaemia patient fan's wish - video
content: Actor Allu Arjun recently brought immense joy to Shraddha, a young girl battling leukaemia, by fulfilling her wish. The duo recreated iconic 'Pushpa' moments, including the signature beard-stroke gesture. Arjun encouraged Shraddha to pursue her dream of becoming a doctor, promising a visit to Hyderabad post-recovery. This heartwarming interaction has deeply resonated with fans online, highlighting the star's compassion.

title: Walking a fundamental right, footpath must for all roads: Supreme Court
content: The Supreme Court has declared the right to walk a fundamental right, integral to life and free movement.  The apex court urged the government to enact a law ensuring well-demarcated footpaths on all roads, prioritizing pedestrian safety over motorized vehicles. This landmark ruling empowers citizens to seek legal recourse for violations and mandates civic bodies to maintain pedestrian infrastructure, transforming urban and rural living.

title: Neeraj Chopra misses podium as Pathirage wins Doha Diamond League title
content: Neeraj Chopra made a strong return to competition at the Doha Diamond League, securing a fourth-place finish with a season-best throw of 85.69 meters. Despite missing a podium spot after an injury layoff, the Olympic champion's performance comfortably met the Commonwealth Games qualification standard. Sri Lanka's Rumesh Tharanga Pathirage clinched the victory with an impressive 88.68 meters, highlighting a competitive field.

title: Eknath Shinde on rebellion: This is just trailer, picture baki hai
content: Maharashtra Deputy CM Eknath Shinde issued a veiled warning to Shiv Sena UBT, stating that the recent departure of six MPs is merely a prelude to further defections. He confidently asserted that his faction is growing, while Shiv Sena UBT is losing its support base. Shinde criticized the opposition's tactics and reiterated his commitment to Hindutva, claiming his party has gained significant strength since the split.

title: PM Modi transfers Rs 2,400 crore under job generation plan
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi highlighted that India's trade agreements with nearly 40 nations are unlocking global markets and professional opportunities. He urged businesses and youth to leverage these avenues, emphasizing that 'quality' in all aspects – from education to products – is crucial for international competitiveness.

title: Centre remains committed to Article 371 safeguards for Ladakh
content: Centre reaffirms commitment to granting Ladakh constitutional safeguards under Article 371 and ensuring bureaucratic accountability to elected leaders, despite reservations from regional representatives over meeting minutes. Discussions are ongoing to define the precise scope of these protections and the framework for administrative oversight, with a customized approach being developed for the region.

title: Indian captain of sanctioned Russian tanker charged in UK; family seeks safe return
content: An Indian captain, Ajay Pant, has been arrested in the UK and charged with breaching sanctions after his Russian oil tanker was intercepted in the English Channel. The vessel, en route to India from Russia, was found to be stateless. Pant's family in Uttarakhand has appealed to the Indian government for his safe return, while authorities confirm consular access has been granted.

title: Nagpur NEET aspirant gets Abu Dhabi as exam centre
content: A Nagpur NEET aspirant received a shocking hall ticket assigning his exam centre to Abu Dhabi, over 2,500 km away, despite not possessing a passport. The National Testing Agency (NTA) assured rectification, but with the exam imminent, the family faced immense stress arranging travel. This incident follows widespread reports of exam centre changes, even for students who opted to retain their original locations, adding to the anxiety post-paper leak.

title: 'Ready to resign': Uddhav's emotional offer amid MPs rebellion, but with a condition
content: Uddhav Thackeray projected confidence amid a fresh party rebellion, assuring Shiv Sainiks are "fired up" despite six Lok Sabha MPs defecting. He apologized to voters for electing lawmakers who switched sides. Thackeray dismissed merger talks with Congress and defended his leadership, highlighting electoral wins. He warned of a threat to democracy with a move towards a "one party, no election" system. This latest crisis follows the 2022 split.

title: Watch: Video shows aftermath of train collision north of London
content: A major train collision north of London on Friday left numerous passengers injured, with some describing a "big bang" and "bomb explosion" impact. Emergency services responded swiftly to the scene south of Bedford. Reports indicate potential for up to 50 casualties, with witnesses detailing severe injuries and widespread distress. Rail unions are closely monitoring the developing situation.

title: Pak man who crossed LoC sent back, handshakes mark handover
content: A Pakistani national, Asad Khan, apprehended last week after crossing the Line of Control in Kupwara, Jammu and Kashmir, has been safely repatriated. Khan, from Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, was detained near Samari village on June 12. The Indian Army stated he was treated with dignity and compassion, with his handover at the Aman Setu peace bridge highlighting professional military conduct and humanitarian values.

title: Europe’s Russia dilemma: Why EU is opening a channel with Moscow despite Ukraine war
content: European Council President Antonio Costa defended his office's direct diplomatic outreach to Russia, emphasizing the need for communication channels despite the absence of immediate peace talks over Ukraine. While some EU leaders expressed concerns about uncoordinated efforts and maintaining pressure on Moscow, others, like Spain and Ireland, backed the initiative as a necessary diplomatic tool to convey the bloc's messages directly to Russia.

title: IPL 2027 Team Moves: Kuldeep Yadav set for LSG; Pant likely to return to DC
content: Kuldeep Yadav is set to join the Lucknow Super Giants for the 2027 IPL season. Meanwhile, Rishabh Pant is strongly expected to make a return to his former team, the Delhi Capitals, with the specifics of his move still being finalised. Both players had a torrid campaign in IPL 2026 for their respective teams.

title: Kerala Revised Budget: In-principle approval for Cheemeni industrial park
content: The project, which is expected to generate around 15,000 employment opportunities, is among a series of development initiatives approved for the Trikaripur constituency

title: Drama at Kerala DHS office as two senior officials claim charge
content: Reena resumes office citing tribunal order, while Additional DHS retains charge pending official instructions

title: "This Is Just A Trailer": Eknath Shinde Amid Team Thackeray Rebellion
content: This duelling comes against the backdrop of a fresh crisis for the Uddhav Thackeray-led Shiv Sena (UBT), with six of its nine Lok Sabha MPs expected to join the rival Shiv Sena led by Eknath Shinde.

title: Nobel Prize Winner John Jumper Quits Google DeepMind For Anthropic
content: John Jumper won the chemistry Nobel Prize alongside Deepmind CEO Demis Hassabis for creating an AI model that can predict the structure of proteins called AlphaFold.

title: "I Was Trying To Be Funny, Not The Boss": Trump On Viral G7 Remark
content: The US president drew laughs on Wednesday from his fellow G7 leaders for his quip as he walked into a meeting on the last day of their summit when he said, "I'm the boss."

title: "Be Quiet": Israeli Envoy, Top UN Official's Shouting Match At New York Meet
content: Israeli envoy Danny Danon clashed with UN official Vanessa Frazier over a report that blacklisted Israel for alleged sexual abuses in conflict.

title: Trump Asked Which World Leader He Admires Most, He Picks PM Modi, Xi Jinping
content: Donald Trump made the remarks on "The Axios Show" on Thursday, shortly after returning from a G7 summit in France that he described as "very dominant".

title: India Secures Historic Vice-Presidency Of Anti-Money Laundering Body FATF
content: Vivek Aggarwal, a 1994-batch Indian Administrative Service (IAS) officer of the Madhya Pradesh cadre and currently serving as Secretary, Ministry of Culture, has been elected and appointed as FATF Vice-President for the term from July 2026 to June 20

title: "Lot Of People Had Broken Legs": Passenger Recounts UK Train Collision Horror
content: One person died and dozens were injured, 11 of them "very seriously", after two passenger trains collided near London on Friday.

title: Char Dham Yatra Pilgrim Footfall Crosses 37 Lakh, Death Count Reaches 198
content: More than 37 lakh pilgrims have visited Uttarakhand since the Char Dham Yatra began in April, while 198 fatalities have been reported among the devotees during the ongoing pilgrimage season, according to official data.

title: Ladakh Bans Single-Use Plastics, Imposes Heavy Penalties For Violations
content: According to the Lieutenant Governor, the checks are aimed at preventing banned plastic products from entering the Union Territory and strengthening enforcement on the ground.

title: "World's Most Luxurious Plane": Trump Unveils Qatar-Gifted Air Force One
content: US President Donald Trump unveiled on Friday a replacement Air Force One aircraft gifted by Qatar, which he dubbed a...

title: Trump Says He Urged Israel To Agree To Ceasefire Deal With Hezbollah
content: In a phone interview with NBC News, Trump said he had been in contact with Israel earlier in the day and encouraged its leadership to support the ceasefire effort.

title: Trump Says He Is Going To Turkey, China This Year
content: US  President Donald Trump said on Friday he will go to Turkey and make another trip to China at some point in 2026.

title: AK-47 Rifle, 26 Pistols Seized Near Indo-Pak Border In Amritsar, 1 Arrested
content: Punjab Director General of Police Gaurav Yadav said preliminary investigation revealed that the accused was acting on the directions of an Australia-based associate, who allegedly shared the weapons' drop location through social media applications.

title: Kantara Actor Rukmini Vasanth Files Complaint Over Morphed Images, 3 Arrested
content: Three persons have been arrested by the Cyber Crime Police Station of Bengaluru City in connection with the circulation of AI-generated objectionable images and videos of actor Rukmini Vasanth.

title: Gulf Nations Now Realise US Alignment Comes At A Cost: Ravi Agrawal
content: Speaking to NDTV's Vishnu Som, Ravi Agrawal, editor-in-chief of Foreign Policy, said the war had become deeply unpopular in the US and many Americans were simply relieved that US President Donald Trump wanted to end American involvement.

title: "PM Modi Great Leader, Stays Out Of Wars, Which Is Smart": Trump
content: US President Donald Trump lavished praise on Prime Minister Narendra Modi, saying he is a "great leader" and "stays out of wars" and the US now does "fair business" with India.

title: "Joy Working With Vijay, He Is Humble, Patient": Tamil Nadu Minister To NDTV
content: A key concern facing Tamil Nadu's higher education sector is that 12 state universities are currently functioning without vice-chancellors.

title: Implementation Of Guarantees To Change, Karnataka To Bring In New Checks
content: One of the key changes under consideration is stricter scrutiny of beneficiaries. The government has found instances where benefits continued to be credited to beneficiaries who have passed away.

title: Stealth Frigate 'Dunagiri', 2 Other Ships To Be Inducted Into Navy on Sunday
content: 'Dunagiri' is equipped with advanced weapons and sensors, including BrahMos surface-to-surface missiles and the Medium Range Surface-to-Air Missile system, significantly enhancing the Navy's combat capabilities.

title: RJD & CPI-ML attack Congress for saying their MLAs cross-voted
content: the pinpricks highlight the internal dissonance among the allies that form the INDIA bloc . the skirmishes have intensified after the largely defunct bloc met last week after a gap of over two years . some of the criticism of Congress has raised questions about its future .

title: 16 arrested in Odisha for assaulting, stripping Delhi woman mistaken for child kidnapper
content: police arrested 16 people who were part of a mob that chased and assaulted a woman from Delhi and her male colleague from Gujarat . the accused also half-stripped the woman . they are involved in a turmeric packaging project in Kandhamal district .

title: Water in 166 major reservoirs across country dips to 28% of storage capacity
content: Vishwa Mohan is Senior Editor at The Times of India. He writes on environment, climate change, agriculture, water resources and clean energy, tracking policy issues and climate diplomacy. He has been covering Parliament since 2003 to see how politics shaped up domestic policy and India’s position at global platform. Before switching over to explore sustainable development issues, Vishwa had covered internal security and investigative agencies for more than a decade. Read More

title: BJP summons top Karnataka netas over cross-voting
content: A Journalist by profession, Sandeep Moudgal has an experience of 18 years in the field with Politics and Policy as forte. He is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Bengaluru bureau and Karnataka as his jurisdiction. Has a Master's degree in Ancient History and Archaeology from Mysore University along with a PGDJ from the Asian College of Journalism. Read More

title: NEET retest: Not only paper, NTA guarding paper-making process too
content: Manash Pratim Gohain is a seasoned journalist with over two decades at The Times of India, where he has built a rich body of work spanning education policy, politics, and governance. Renowned for his incisive coverage of the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020, accreditation reforms, and skilling initiatives, he has also reported on student politics, urban policy, and social movements. His political reportage—both reflective and news-driven—adds depth to his writing, bridging policy with public impact. Through his 2,500 articles and related outlets, he has emerged as a trusted voice in national discourse, particularly in linking education reform to broader societal change. Read More

title: SC proposes statutory fund to aid first-generation lawyers, will examine women's infra issues
content: a bench led by CJI Surya Kant proposed creation of a statutory fund . a group of women lawyers led by senior advocate monika Gusain said a majority of court complexes lack women-friendly infrastructure . the meagre income/stipend given to young first-generation advocates also force the meritorious to leave the litigation-oriented practice .

title: Bengal cops start probe into TMC's bank accounts
content: Tamaghna Banerjee, a reporter from Kolkata, covers crime, aviation, human rights and politics. He has a keen interest in human interest and rural reporting. He has done his postgraduation in journalism and mass communication. He has a total of 14 years in journalism. Read More

title: The chokepoint doctrine: How the Iran war exposed the rise of middle powers
content: john avlon: how long the united states and Iran have given themselves to negotiate a final deal? he says the answer is not ideology, not a nuclear deterrent, not even military strength . he asks: what is a middle power? middle powers do not rise through military supremacy or ideological appeal .

title: Telegram ban minimum that government could do: Delhi high court
content: As legal editor for Delhi, Abhinav Garg handles coverage of courts and connected legal challenges shaping the capital. From breaking down complex law related jargon to simplifying how a particular verdict or development in courts may impact the readers, Abhinav brings with him over two decades of experience in the field.
